channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Overwatch Reveals What Its Newest Hero Looks Like And Fans Think They Know Who It Is
item.content:encoded:
Overwatch’s next season is just a few weeks away, and Blizzard has already said it’s releasing a new hero every season in 2026. On Tuesday, the company revealed the game’s next hero with just a single illustration. However, there are enough clues in this art and from Blizzard’s Overwatch reboot plans that fans think they have a pretty good idea who it is.

Blizzard is going to reveal the next hero in earnest with a full trailer on April 8 at 12:00 p.m. ET, but the company has filled in her silhouette in the hero line-up it’s been using to promote the game’s relaunch. We see a woman with long, braided hair wearing a beret and a cropped green vest. She’s also wielding a rifle and has a pink drone flying next to her. 

Respectfully… y’all aren’t ready for this one 😮‍💨🔥

Join us Apr 8 at 9am PT for the premiere of our latest Hero Trailer as we kick off the next chapter in the Reign of Talon 💪 pic.twitter.com/1Etxn68tax

— Overwatch (@PlayOverwatch) March 31, 2026

She looks dope, but that’s not much to go on for the average Overwatch fan. However, if you’re a sicko who’s been keeping up with all the extended lore, you know all of those features add up to sound like Frankie, a character who debuted in the Overwatch novel Deadlock Rebels back in 2021. 

Frankie hasn’t come up in Overwatch lore much, but Blizzard has said that each hero that debuts over the next year will be tied to the ongoing story, and the Deadlock Gang that Frankie belongs to is wrapped up in Vendetta’s crusade, which is at the center of this year’s Talon-centric arc. 

So everything we know points to this being Frankie. We’ll know more when Blizzard reveals her properly next week.



https://kotaku.com/overwatch-new-hero-51-frankie-deadlock-gang-ashe-2000683600
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Supergirl Only Has Three Days To Save Her Dog’s Life In Newly Released Trailer
item.content:encoded:
The new trailer for Warner Bros. and DC’s upcoming Supergirl movie gives us our first extended look at Jason Momoa’s Lobo in action and also reveals that the movie’s plot is all about Kara/Supergirl, played by Milly Alcock, trying to save Kyrpto, her super-powered dog, from dying after he’s poisoned by nasty villains.

I’ll be honest, I wasn’t sure if I was going to be super invested in yet another comic book superhero movie. But damn it, DC and WB have figured out a surefire way to get me and most of the internet to care about yet another superhero flick: Give the main hero the goal of saving her pup. Yeah, okay, I’m on board for watching Kara kick ass and take names to save Krypto and get the cure he needs before he dies in three days. Here’s the new trailer:

Both Kara and Krypto were first seen in last year’s excellent Superman movie from director and DC films boss James Gunn. Kyrpto was an instant hit with basically everyone, and now DC is threatening to kill the pup in this new trailer. I’d be shocked if the pup didn’t make it out alive at the end of this, but I’m still going to get teary-eyed watching Kara struggle with her dying pup when Supergirl lands in theaters on June 26.



https://kotaku.com/supergirl-only-has-three-days-to-save-her-dogs-life-in-newly-released-trailer-2000683591
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: A Tease For A Nier Cosmic Horror Project On April Fools’ Day Is Making Fans Lose It
item.content:encoded:
If you’re reading this in most parts of the world, it’s March 31. For Japan, however, it’s April 1, April Fools’ Day, or what news writers call “the most annoying day of the year.” A lot of video game companies love to post jokes and tease non-existent projects as part of the holiday, though sometimes companies like Sega do something substantial like release a whole-ass video game. Now that Japan is firmly in April Fools’ Day, the Japanese Nier social media account is teasing a “Cosmic Horror” project in the RPG series, and if there’s anyone in the video game industry that might tease something in earnest on April 1, it’s Nier director Yoko Taro.

【NieR 新プロジェクト 制作血定】
虚無に生まれるは、希望か。
それとも、救済という名の絶望か。

━━━Coming Soon…… pic.twitter.com/nBdtqQEF12

— NieR公式PRアカウント (@NieR_JPN) March 31, 2026

The “teaser” doesn’t reveal much. The video features art showing some kind of robotic creature with a tendril for a head and caked-up cheeks. We can also see a sketch of Emil, the young boy turned super-weapon from the previous games who wears a stone mask with a creepy smile, in the background. All of this comes after Square Enix has teased a new Nier project, but given that it’s April Fools’ Day, this is probably not whatever Taro is cooking up…unless…?

It’s been almost a decade since Nier Automata launched in 2017, but Nier fans haven’t been completely starved for anything new. The game keeps crossing over with any game that asks, the concert series gave fans an epilogue of sorts for Automata, the anime adaptation introduced some new story elements, and there was a Nier mobile game called Reincarnation that included substantial worldbuilding…and that Square Enix took offline in 2024. Whatever Square is cooking up, it would be cool if they gave Reincarnation the Octopath Traveler 0 treatment and stripped out all the gacha nonsense to make it playable on other platforms.



https://kotaku.com/nier-new-game-tease-yoko-taro-announcement-cosmic-horror-2000683585
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
